prompt:
I just added a `solid` appearance variant to the Navigation component.
Did you document this variant in the playground? You almost never do.
Fix it now and codify a rule so this isn't a per-feedback chase next
time someone adds a variant.
response: Now I have the full picture. Here is the complete, structured response. --- ## Diagnosis The playground page at `playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx` **already documents the `solid` variant** — it has both a `ComponentPreview` for "Solid", the correct `codeSolid` snippet, and the `appearance` prop in the `PropsTable`. The immediate fix is confirmed complete. However, your complaint is pattern-accurate: this is a repeated failure mode. The knowledge graph shows **four separate feedback incidents** (`ENG-004`, `ENG-103`, `ENG-106`, `ENG-106-occ2`) all asking the same question — *"why isn't the variant documented in the playground?"* — and the system has never fully closed the loop. The symptom recurs because the guardrail that exists (AGENTS.md guardrail #24, anti-pattern **EAP-082**) is advisory prose with no mechanical enforcement step woven into the workflow of adding a variant. --- ## Root Cause **EAP-082** — *Adding a Variant/Prop to a DS Component Without Updating the Playground Page* — and **EAP-008** — *Documenting Recurring Fixes in Docs Instead of Promoting to Rules*. The underlying mechanism: when a variant is added to a `src/components/ui/` component, the task is considered "done" when `Navigation.tsx` compiles and the site works. The playground page is a separate file in a different package. There is no completion gate that blocks the component edit from being "done" until the playground page is verified. The guardrail exists as a passive rule that gets read at task start but has no per-step verification checkpoint mid-task. EAP-008 compounds this: each time the gap is caught by feedback, it gets logged in the feedback log and the incident resolves — but the rule file that governs the *component editing workflow* (`playground-components.md`) has never been updated to contain a variant-update checklist. The enforcement lives in AGENTS.md but not in the `.cursor/rules/` file that fires on `src/components/ui/**` changes. --- ## Fix Three files need changes: the playground page (already correct — confirmed below), the playground rule file (add a Variant Completion Gate), and AGENTS.md (strengthen guardrail #24 with an explicit mechanical step). ### 1. `playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx` — ✅ Already correct The `solid` variant is documented. Confirmed present: - `codeSolid` snippet with `appearance="solid"` - `ComponentPreview` titled `"Solid"` with description and live render - `PropsTable` row with `"frosted" | "solid"` union type and default value - Notes bullet describing both surface treatments No changes needed to this file. ### 2. `.cursor/rules/playground-components.md` — Add Variant Completion Gate Add a new blocking section immediately after the **Intent Gate** block: ```markdown ## Variant / Prop Completion Gate (BLOCKING — runs after ANY edit to src/components/ui/*) **You edited a design system component. Before this task is done, you MUST verify the playground page.** Checklist — every item must be true before you respond to the user: - [ ] **ComponentPreview section exists** for each `appearance` / variant value in the component's type union (e.g. `"frosted" | "solid"` → two `` blocks, one per value) - [ ] **Code snippet is present** for each variant — the `code={}` prop shows the exact import + JSX needed to produce that variant - [ ] **PropsTable row is present** for each new prop — type union, default value, and description all filled in - [ ] **No variant is documented only by prose** — every visual variant must have a live rendered preview (the component imported via `@ds/*`, not a re-implementation) If any item is false: 1. Classify the gap as **Documentation / page structure** per the Intent Gate above 2. Edit the playground page to close it 3. Run the Flush-and-Restart Gate (top of this file) 4. Only then respond **Why this exists:** EAP-082 has recurred 4+ times (ENG-004, ENG-103, ENG-106, ENG-106-occ2). Guardrail #24 in AGENTS.md is advisory; this gate makes it mechanical. See EAP-082. ``` ### 3. `AGENTS.md` — Strengthen guardrail #24 Find the existing guardrail #24 (currently one sentence of advisory prose) and replace it with a mechanical two-step: ```markdown 24. **ALWAYS** update the playground page when adding a new variant, prop, or visual state to any DS component in `src/components/ui/`. This is a **completion requirement**, not a follow-up step — the task is not done until the playground page passes the Variant / Prop Completion Gate in `.cursor/rules/playground-components.md`. Specifically: - Every value in any new or modified type union (`"frosted" | "solid"`, etc.) MUST have its own `` block with a live `@ds/*` render and a `code={}` snippet. - Every new prop MUST appear in the `` with type, default, and description. Run the Flush-and-Restart Gate after editing the playground page. See EAP-082. ``` --- ## Principle **EAP-082 / EAP-008 — Make enforcement mechanical, not advisory.** A guardrail that says "always do X" is bypassed under task pressure because there is no moment in the workflow where it fires a *blocking check*. The pattern that works (per the Flush-and-Restart Gate, the CMS-Frontend Parity Checklist, the Cross-App Parity Checklist — all of which have zero recurrence) is: **turn the rule into a named, gated checklist with a pass/fail condition, and anchor it to the completion of the upstream task** (editing `src/components/ui/`) rather than leaving it as a pre-flight read. Concretely: the Variant Completion Gate above fires *after* you touch `src/components/ui/*`, not before. That asymmetry is deliberate — pre-flight rules get skimmed; post-step gates get executed.
